channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Palestinians urge support for UN resolutions demanding an immediate Gaza ceasefire
item.description: The Palestinians have urged U.N. member nations to support resolutions demanding an immediate ceasefire in Gaza and backing the U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees that Israel has moved to ban.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israeli strikes on Gaza kill at least 33 including children, Palestinian medics say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes pounded the Gaza Strip overnight and into Wednesday, with one attack ripping through a home where displaced people were sheltering in the isolated north. The strikes killed at least 33 people including children, Palestinian health officials said.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump organization officially launches new Saudi Arabia tower
item.description: Eric Trump, executive vice president of the Trump Organization, officially announced the company’s first project in Saudi Arabia on Wednesday, which consists of a 47 floor building.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Hundreds of tractors block central London streets as farmers protest tax change
item.description: An inheritance tax of up to 20 percent will be applied to British farms in 2026. Hundreds of farmers protested the new policy by blocking the streets of London with tractors Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gabbard tests trust of allies as Trump’s potential intel director
item.description: Controversy over President-elect Trump’s pick for intelligence chief, former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), is raising uncomfortable questions for America’s closest allies over whether the U.S. will remain a trusted partner for sharing critical, sensitive information.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Syrians flock to morgues looking for loved ones who perished in Assad's prisons
item.description: Many bodies have been found in Syrian detention centers and prisons since President Bashar al-Assad's government fell.
item.dc:creator: Hussein Malla And Omar Albam, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FIFA names Saudi Arabia as 2034 World Cup host; Spain, Portugal and Morocco to co-host 2030 edition
item.description: ZURICH (AP) — Saudi Arabia was officially confirmed Wednesday by FIFA as host of the 2034 World Cup in men’s soccer, giving the oil-rich kingdom its biggest prize yet for massive spending on global sports driven by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: America's closest ally in Syria is losing ground as a new order takes shape
item.description: The jihadi rebels who toppled Syrian President Bashar Assad say they want to build a unified, inclusive country. But after 14 years of civil war, putting that ideal into practice will not be easy.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: These Palestinians disappeared after encounters with Israeli troops in Gaza
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Reem Ajour says she last saw her husband and then 4-year-old daughter in March, when Israeli soldiers raided a family home in northern Gaza.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SARAH EL DEEB and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Immigration surge under Biden is largest in US history: NYT
item.description: The immigration surge under President Biden has been the largest in U.S. history, The New York Times reported Wednesday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: An explosion in the Afghan capital kills the Taliban refugee minister
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — A suicide bombing in the Afghan capital on Wednesday killed the Taliban refugee minister and two others, officials said, in the most brazen attack on a member of the Taliban inner circle since they returned to power three years ago.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken testifies at hearing on Afghanistan withdrawal
item.description: Lawmakers have been demanding answers into how such a significant operation could have gone so wrong.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Scholz sets Germany on course for an early election as he requests a confidence vote next week
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Chancellor Olaf Scholz formally set Germany on course for an early election Wednesday by requesting a confidence vote in parliament next week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supporters of Bangladesh Nationalist Party hold protest march as tensions continue with India
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Thousands of members of youth and student bodies belonging to the Bangladesh Nationalist Party on Wednesday held a road march procession from Bangladesh’s capital toward its eastern border with India to protest against an attack on a diplomatic mission earlier this month and
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mike Huckabee 'commends' Biden support for Israel's self-defense
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, The Hill

item.title: Organized crime gangs expanded into a third of cities in Brazil’s Amazon, report finds
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Criminal gangs are operating in over a third of municipalities in Brazil’s Amazon rainforest driving a boom in violence, according to a report published Wednesday by a prominent nonprofit organization.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli warplanes pound Syria as troops reportedly advance deeper into the country
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Israel carried out a wave of heavy airstrikes across Syria as its troops advanced deeper into the country, a Syrian opposition war monitor said Tuesday, and the Israeli defense minister announced that his forces had destroyed Syria’s navy.
item.dc:creator: ABDULRAHMAN SHAHEEN and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rights group demands probe of ex-governor, 2 army officers in Congo for possible humanitarian crimes
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Amnesty International called for an investigation Wednesday into a former governor and two top army officials in Congo for “possible crimes against humanity” during a deadly crackdown on demonstrators last year protesting the U.N. peacekeeping mission in the country.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mike Huckabee 'commends' Biden support for Israel's self-defense
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu vows to challenge 'absurd' corruption charges as he takes the stand in his trial
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promised defiantly on Tuesday to knock down corruption allegations against him as he began testifying in his long-running trial, becoming the first sitting Israeli leader to take the stand as a criminal defendant.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel bombs hundreds of sites across Syria as army pushes into border zone
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours, targeting “most of the strategic weapons stockpiles” in the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-defense chief in South Korea tried to kill himself after being arrested over martial law
item.description: South Korea’s previous defense minister was stopped from attempting suicide while in detention over last week’s martial law declaration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US loans Ukraine $20B from seized Russian assets
item.description: The Treasury Department gave Ukraine a $20 billion loan funded by seized Russian assets Tuesday as part of a broader Group of Seven (G7) effort to boost the country's defense. The money will be transmitted to Ukraine through the World Bank, which received the loan from the U.S. on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, The Hill

item.title: 1,500-year-old Ten Commandments tablet set for auction
item.description: The tablet has twenty lines of text but only nine of the commandments, with "Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord in vain" omitted.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Israel strikes targets in Syria after Assad regime falls
item.description: Israeli forces carried out airstrikes on targets in Syria as Israel continues to take more aggressive action in the country following the collapse of Bashar Assad's regime over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: At least 8 killed in building collapse in Cairo
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — An apartment building collapsed on Tuesday in Egypt’s capital, killing at least eight people, authorities said. The Health Ministry said in a statement that the collapse of the six-story building in Cairo’s western neighborhood of Waili also injured three people, who were hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden seems to take credit for Assad's downfall amid fears of Islamic State revival
item.description: President Biden angered some critics when he seemed to claim his administration's Mideast policies led to the downfall of Syria's Bashar Assad on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour ends by shattering own record, grossing an estimated $2.2B, Pollstar says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour brought in $2.2 billion in its nearly two-year run, making it the highest-grossing tour of all time for a second year in a row, Pollstar estimates from data collected across 149 shows.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fall of Syria's Bashar Assad is strategic blow to Iran and Russia, experts say
item.description: Analysts and commentators say the collapse of Syria's Bashar al-Assad's regime is likely to reshape the power dynamics in the Middle East while signaling a critical shift in influence among world powers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea imposes a travel ban on President Yoon over martial law declaration
item.description: The South Korean Justice Ministry imposed an overseas travel ban against President Yoon Suk Yeol on Monday over his martial law declaration last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ousted Syrian leader Assad flees to Moscow after fall of Damascus, Russian state media say
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Ousted Syrian leader Bashar Assad fled to Moscow and received asylum from his longtime ally, Russian media said Sunday, hours after a stunning rebel advance seized control of Damascus and ended his family’s 50 years of iron rule.
item.dc:creator: ABDULRAHMAN SHAHEEN, SARAH EL DEEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New reports claim UNRWA works with terrorists, teaches hate as agency hits back at critics
item.description: UNRWA is facing new allegations that its former and present leadership have met with Hamas and other terror-related groups. This comes as another NGO says agency-run schools continue to teach hate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump back on the world stage at Notre Dame reopening in Paris
item.description: Zelenskyy said his first face-to-face encounter with Trump since the election was “good and productive,” adding they had agreed to continue working together.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: United Kingdom cracks down on junk food TV advertisements
item.description: The United Kingdom's Department of Health estimates that 7.2 billion calories will be removed from the diets of British children.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Donald Trump's incoming team met with Ukraine officials
item.description: Trump's team is trying to bring Ukraine and Russia to the negotiating table quickly, potentially through diplomatic coercion.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Amnesty International says Israel committing genocide in Gaza. Israel rejects allegations
item.description: The offensive is among the deadliest and most destructive since World War II, and has destroyed vast areas of the besieged coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
